app.title: Piauí para o Mundo - Tourism & Culture Guide
app.subtitle: Explore the state's cultural diversity
highlights.title: Highlights
spots.title: Tourist spots
creative.subtitle: Startups, digital innovation and local solutions driving Piauí forward
region.costaDoDelta.name: Delta Coast Hub
region.costaDoDelta.summary: Discover the natural beauty of Piauí's coast, with scenic beaches and maritime heritage.
region.poloDasOrigens.name: Origins Hub - Serra da Capivara
region.poloDasOrigens.summary: Travel through prehistory with archaeological sites and preserved natural parks.
region.teresina.name: Teresina Hub
region.teresina.summary: The cultural capital: museums, rivers and events celebrating Piauí's history.
spot.portoLuisCorreia.title: Port of Luís Correia
spot.portoLuisCorreia.summary: Historic port connecting Piauí to the Atlantic world since colonial times.
spot.canyonRioPoti.title: Poti River Canyon
spot.canyonRioPoti.summary: Impressive geological formation with a breathtaking landscape.
spot.parque7Cidades.title: Sete Cidades National Park
spot.parque7Cidades.summary: A set of unique rock formations with ancestral myths and legends.
spot.serraDaCapivara.title: Serra da Capivara
spot.serraDaCapivara.summary: World-class archaeological site with evidence of ancient human occupation.
spot.deltaDoParnaiba.title: Parnaíba Delta
spot.deltaDoParnaiba.summary: Archipelago formed by five outlets to the sea, with endangered species and scenic islands.
spot.cachoeiraUrubu.title: Urubu Waterfall Ecological Park
spot.cachoeiraUrubu.summary: Lush waterfall with fast falls and the piracema phenomenon.
spot.praiaBarraGrande.title: Barra Grande Beach
spot.praiaBarraGrande.summary: Ideal beach for kitesurfing, with strong winds and shallow waters.
spot.praiaPedraSal.title: Pedra do Sal Beach
spot.praiaPedraSal.summary: Beach divided by a hill, with strong waves and calmer areas.
spot.lagoaPortinho.title: Portinho Lagoon
spot.lagoaPortinho.summary: Lagoon with dark waters and pale sand, ideal for water sports.
spot.praiaCoqueiro.title: Coqueiro Beach
spot.praiaCoqueiro.summary: Beach with clear waters and natural pools formed by reefs.
spot.serraConfusoes.title: Serra das Confusões National Park
spot.serraConfusoes.summary: Brazil's largest caatinga reserve, with landscapes that change color.
spot.liraEcoParque.title: Lira Eco Park
spot.liraEcoParque.summary: Park with activities such as tree climbing, zip lining and kayaking.
spot.miranteGritador.title: Gritador Viewpoint
spot.miranteGritador.summary: Panoramic 720-meter viewpoint with native vegetation and rocky walls.
spot.cachoeiraSaltoLiso.title: Salto Liso Waterfall
spot.cachoeiraSaltoLiso.summary: Waterfall with a 35-meter drop, accessible by trail.
spot.caldeiraoRodrigues.title: Caldeirão dos Rodrigues
spot.caldeiraoRodrigues.summary: Archaeological site with rock paintings and an enchanting plateau.
spot.cachoeiraEngenhoVelho.title: Engenho Velho Waterfall
spot.cachoeiraEngenhoVelho.summary: Waterfall with an almost 50-meter drop and stunning cascades.
spot.pedraCastelo.title: Pedra do Castelo Municipal Natural Park
spot.pedraCastelo.summary: Castle-shaped rock formation with prehistoric paintings.
spot.cachoeiraTingidor.title: Tingidor Waterfall
spot.cachoeiraTingidor.summary: Waterfall with bluish waters and a natural pool.
spot.rioPoti.title: Poti River
spot.rioPoti.summary: River that crosses Teresina and helps tell the capital's relationship with its waters.
spot.museuDoPiaui.title: Piauí Museum
spot.museuDoPiaui.summary: Historical and cultural collection dedicated to Piauí's memory.
spot.encontroRios.title: Encontro dos Rios Environmental Park
spot.encontroRios.summary: Meeting point of the Poti and Parnaíba rivers, with legends and gastronomy.
spot.parqueFlorestaFossil.title: Fossil Forest Park
spot.parqueFlorestaFossil.summary: Fossil forest with petrified trunks dating back 250 million years.
creative.artesanatoMarajoara.title: Delta Bobbin Lace
creative.artesanatoMarajoara.description: A traditional craft kept alive by lace makers from Ilha Grande, with handmade pieces that carry the memory and identity of Piauí's coast.
creative.praiasGastronomia.title: Beach Gastronomy
creative.praiasGastronomia.description: Fresh seafood dishes and family recipes passed down through generations.
creative.arteRupestre.title: Rock Art
creative.arteRupestre.description: Paintings and engravings up to 25,000 years old revealing the mysteries of prehistory.
creative.cuisineNordestina.title: Northeastern Cuisine
creative.cuisineNordestina.description: Authentic flavors that reflect the agricultural and cultural richness of the backlands.
creative.economiaCriativaTeresina.title: Technological Progress
creative.economiaCriativaTeresina.descri: Piauí is accelerating its digital agenda with tech hubs, startups and innovation infrastructure.
creative.festasPopulares.title: Bumba Meu Boi
creative.festasPopulares.description: Popular festivals that celebrate Piauí's culture with music, dance and the traditional Bumba Meu Boi performance.
creative.eventosTeresina.title: Teresina Cultural Events
creative.eventosTeresina.description:
Main attractions and programming in the capital:
• Samba at the bandstand and local markets at Pedro II and Saraiva squares.
• June festivals and traditional folk celebrations.
• Summer beach events and leisure programming.
• Museums and theaters with ongoing programming.
• Free concerts (Alok, Marina Sena, Kanalha).
itinerary.description: Select tourist spots to build a cultural route through Piauí.
event.festivalInvernoPedroII.title: Pedro II Winter Festival
event.festivalInvernoPedroII.description: The largest cultural festival in Piauí's mountains, with concerts, exhibitions and an opal fair.
event.semanaBumbaMeuBoi.title: Bumba Meu Boi Week
event.semanaBumbaMeuBoi.description: Folkloric celebration with bumba meu boi parades through the streets of Teresina.
event.encontroSaberesCapivara.title: Capivara Knowledge Gathering
event.encontroSaberesCapivara.descriptio: Academic and cultural event about archaeology and the history of Piauí's backlands.
artisan.ceramicaDelta.title: Delta Artisan Pottery
artisan.ceramicaDelta.description: Unique pieces inspired by the fauna of the Parnaíba Delta, made with ancestral techniques.
artisan.cajuinaArtesanal.title: Artisan Cajuína
artisan.cajuinaArtesanal.description: Traditional Piauí drink made by hand with fresh cashew fruit.
artisan.rendaBilro.title: Bobbin Lace
artisan.rendaBilro.description: Traditional bobbin lace technique creating delicate and unique patterns.
artisan.joiasOpala.title: Opal Jewelry
artisan.joiasOpala.description: Artisan jewelry with precious opals from Pedro II.
artisan.pacocaCarne.title: Sun-Dried Meat Paçoca
artisan.pacocaCarne.description: Backlands delicacy made with shredded sun-dried meat and cassava flour.
artisan.tapecariaRupestre.title: Rock Art Tapestry
artisan.tapecariaRupestre.description: Tapestries inspired by rock paintings from Serra da Capivara.
territoryExplorer.heroEmphasis: The best kept secret of Brazil's Northeast.
territoryExplorer.heroDescription: Piauí adds value through archaeology, nature and food. Three unique pillars no other place in the world offers.
territoryExplorer.searchPlaceholder: Search places, biomes, fruits of Piauí...
territoryExplorer.biomeText: Caatinga, Cerrado, Mata dos Cocais and Atlantic Forest meet here, shaping fruits such as buriti, pequi, cashew and umbu.
territoryExplorer.whyPiauiSubtitle: Travelers come for what only Piauí has: archaeology, wind sports and bold regional cuisine.
territoryExplorer.whyPiauiCard1Title: Archaeology and the origin of the American human
territoryExplorer.whyPiauiCard1Descripti: See the world's largest concentration of rock art at Serra da Capivara and travel through human history.
territoryExplorer.whyPiauiCard2Descripti: The Parnaíba Delta and Barra Grande are global hotspots for kitesurfers and adventure travelers.
territoryExplorer.whyPiauiCard3Descripti: Cashew, buriti, carne de sol and capote deliver a bold, exclusive sensory journey.
territoryExplorer.routeTip: Click on a point to see details and build your environmental itinerary.
territoryExplorer.noSearchResult: I didn't find an exact result, but I brought the territory area for you to explore.
territoryExplorer.biomeSectionSubtitle: Learn about nature, landscapes and cities that live each biome.

territoryExplorer.ambientRegion.label: Vale do Sambito
territoryExplorer.ambientRegion.summary: Dense cerrado, hidden waterfalls and exuberant fauna.
territoryExplorer.ambientRegion.details: An imagined route to connect cerrado, fresh water, fauna observation and small interior communities.

biome.caatinga.title: Caatinga
biome.caatinga.description: Dry sertão vegetation, with twisted shrubs, cacti and exposed earth under a harsh sun in a drought-tolerant ecosystem.
biome.cerrado.title: Cerrado
biome.cerrado.description: Open savannas and gallery forests with grasses, shrubs and typical cerrado wildlife such as deer and dry-season birds.
biome.mataDosCocais.title: Mata dos Cocais
biome.mataDosCocais.description: A transition forest dominated by carnauba and babassu palms, forming a dense green mosaic of palm groves.
biome.mataAtlantica.title: Mata Atlântica
biome.mataAtlantica.description: A coastal stretch of Atlantic Forest, showing remnant forest patches and cleared areas from deforestation.
city.oeiras.name: Oeiras
city.oeiras.nickname: Capital of Faith and first capital of Piauí
city.oeiras.details: Oeiras preserves one of the most symbolic historic centers in the state. It is remembered as the first capital, a cultural heritage site and a center of traditional religious celebrations.
city.picos.name: Picos
city.picos.details: Picos stands out for its strategic location at a highway crossroads and for honey production, connecting trade, services and semiarid landscapes.
city.teresina.name: Teresina
city.teresina.region: Between the Poti and Parnaíba rivers
city.teresina.details: Teresina brings together markets, museums, urban parks and a strong relationship with the Poti and Parnaíba rivers, while serving as the state administrative center.
city.parnaiba.name: Parnaíba
city.parnaiba.details: Parnaíba is one of the main gateways to the Parnaíba Delta, a destination shaped by rivers, islands, dunes, mangroves and traditional communities.
city.saoRaimundoNonato.name: São Raimundo Nonato
city.saoRaimundoNonato.details: São Raimundo Nonato is a base for experiences connected to Serra da Capivara, the Museum of the American Man and routes focused on archaeology and caatinga.
city.pedroII.name: Pedro II
city.pedroII.details: Pedro II combines mountain weather, mineral crafts, music and ecotourism, with highlights including opals, a winter festival and waterfalls.
city.piripiri.name: Piripiri
city.piripiri.details: Piripiri is close to recreation areas, trails and waterfalls in northern Piauí, connecting nature tourism, regional commerce and popular culture.
city.floriano.name: Floriano
city.floriano.details: Floriano is a regional hub in southern Piauí, with a close connection to the Parnaíba River, services, commerce and riverside life.
citySection.faith.title: Faith, history and memory
citySection.faith.description: Cities that preserve the cultural, religious and political formation of Piauí.
citySection.routes.title: Routes, trade and production
citySection.routes.description: Urban centers that move roads, markets, services and the creative economy.
citySection.nature.title: Nature and living heritage
citySection.nature.description: Gateways to parks, waterfalls, the coast, opals and unique landscapes.
invest.title: Investe Piauí & Culture
invest.subtitle: A calendar of public culture, tourism, and business investments with a short path to state transparency portals.
invest.demoDisclaimer: Figures and dates here are sample content for the layout: wire a validated API, open data, or scraping pipeline for production.
invest.monthNumbers.title: Monthly numbers
invest.chart.title: Cultural investment trend (illustrative)
invest.chart.sub: Monthly amounts in BRL — series produced or enriched by the feed script.
invest.search.label: Smart event search
invest.search.placeholder: e.g. tech events, delta, open calls…
invest.search.hint: Local search uses words and synonyms (e.g. “technology” can surface the innovation forum). Optional: Gemini with the same key as the assistant.
invest.search.geminiFail: AI ranking failed; local search still applies.
invest.search.geminiNeedKey: Set VITE_GEMINI_API_KEY to enable AI search.
invest.block.events.title: Upcoming events
invest.block.events.sub: Shows, festivals, fairs, and calls — referencing Investe Piauí and SECULT (State Government).
invest.block.thermo.title: Investment thermometer
invest.block.thermo.sub: Quick indicators to scan volume and opportunities (demo).
invest.block.map.title: Tourism map in focus
invest.block.map.sub: Destinations with recent upgrades or events on the coast and in the south-central region.
invest.filter.empty: No events for this filter. Pick "All" or another city.
invest.metric.monthCommitment.label: Illustrative total for events & culture (month)
invest.metric.openCalls.label: Open calls & notices (sample)
invest.metric.municipalities.label: Municipalities with culture or tourism actions (sample)
invest.map.delta.title: Coast & Delta
invest.map.delta.summary: Beaches, islands, and coastal routes with infrastructure and seasonal events.
invest.map.picos.title: Picos hub
invest.map.picos.summary: Fairs, logistics, and popular culture in the south-central area — jump to the map for municipalities.
invest.event.hubTeresina.title: Investe Piauí hub meetup
invest.event.hubTeresina.summary: Business and innovation round focused on startups and institutional partnerships.
invest.event.festivalInverno.title: Winter Festival (highlands)
invest.event.festivalInverno.summary: Major highlands culture calendar supporting local artists and tourism.
invest.event.editalPicos.title: SECULT call — regional circuit
invest.event.editalPicos.summary: Workshops and cultural showcases with registrations around Picos.
invest.event.rotaLitoral.title: Coastal business route
invest.event.rotaLitoral.summary: Tourism and trade fair focused on Parnaíba and delta municipalities.
invest.event.feiraInovacao.title: Open innovation fair
invest.event.feiraInovacao.summary: Digital solutions and creative economy demos in the capital.
itinerary.interestsHint: Pick themes (ecology, history, schools, etc.) to filter places. With none selected, all places in the chosen territory appear.
lab.title: Innovation lab
lab.subtitle: Assistant, quiz gamification, and a space for territory suggestions.
lab.quizIntro: Three questions to reinforce how you read the territory. You earn points when you beat your previous score.
assistant.error: Temporary fallback reply while the main assistant is unavailable:
assistant.welcome: Hi! I'm your Piauí Virtual Guide. Ask about itineraries, biomes, history, culture or food to plan your trip with confidence.
assistant.quickQuestion1: What to do in Pedro II over a weekend?
assistant.quickQuestion2: What is the best time to visit the Parnaíba Delta?
assistant.quickQuestion3: Tell me the history and curiosities of Serra da Capivara National Park.
assistant.actionPrompt:
Where do we go next? Type the number of your choice:
[1] Add this destination to my itinerary.
[2] Discover where to eat and stay nearby.
[3] Change route and explore another territory.
assistant.reply.empty: Write a short question so I can try to help.
assistant.reply.genericLogistics: Best season: May to October, with drier weather and gentle temperatures. Bring light clothes and sunscreen.
assistant.reply.genericItinerary: Morning: start at one main attraction. Afternoon: visit a nearby natural or cultural site. Evening: explore regional food.
assistant.reply.cityLogistics: The best time to visit {{city}} is May to October. Bring sun protection, light clothes, and water.
assistant.reply.cityItinerary: Morning: visit the main highlights. Afternoon: explore a local attraction or trail. Evening: taste regional cuisine.
assistant.reply.food: Piauí cuisine highlights seafood, sun-dried beef, freshwater fish, and mountain dishes. On the coast choose fish and shrimp; inland try carne de sol and cassava.
assistant.reply.foodLogistics: In coastal regions like Parnaíba and Luís Correia, look for restaurants with fresh seafood options. Inland, try dishes with cassava and carne de sol.
assistant.reply.foodItinerary: Morning: visit a local market or food stall. Afternoon: taste a regional dish in a restaurant. Evening: try a traditional dessert or drink.
assistant.reply.lodging: In Piauí, cities such as Teresina, Parnaíba, Pedro II, and São Raimundo Nonato offer traveler-friendly lodging.
assistant.reply.lodgingLogistics: Book accommodation near top sights: coast in Parnaíba, archaeology in São Raimundo Nonato, mountain scenery in Pedro II.
assistant.reply.lodgingItinerary: Morning: confirm your reservation. Afternoon: plan transport to the destination. Evening: rest and prepare for the next day.
assistant.reply.season: The best time to travel to Piauí is the dry season from May to October, with less rain and more open trails.
assistant.reply.seasonLogistics: Avoid heavier rains from January to April. From May to October, pack light clothing, sun protection, and a reusable water bottle.
assistant.reply.seasonItinerary: Morning: focus on outdoor activities. Afternoon: choose nature tours. Evening: explore indoor cultural experiences.
assistant.reply.defaultLogistics: Prefer the dry season from May to October and pack light clothing, sun protection and water. Avoid heavy rains from January to April.
assistant.reply.defaultItinerary: Morning: choose your main Piauí destination. Afternoon: select the nearest top points. Evening: book lodging and meals.
assistant.reply.deltaLogistics: Best season: July to December, with wind for kitesurfing and drier conditions. Bring sunscreen, a hat and light clothes.
assistant.reply.deltaItinerary: Morning: boat tour in the Delta. Afternoon: kite or stand-up paddle in Barra Grande. Evening: seafood dinner in Parnaíba.
assistant.reply.capivaraLogistics: Best season: June to October, with less rain and safer trails. Wear closed shoes, a hat, and carry plenty of water.
assistant.reply.capivaraItinerary: Morning: visit the Museum of the American Man. Afternoon: explore archaeological trails. Evening: return to São Raimundo Nonato.
assistant.reply.itineraryItinerary: Morning: start with one main point. Afternoon: connect a nearby attraction. Evening: plan lodging and meals.
assistant.reply.educationItinerary: Morning: field trip in the Origins pole. Afternoon: combine trail and museum. Evening: reflect on local history and culture.
assistant.reply.municipalitiesItinerary: Morning: visit a historic center. Afternoon: go to a natural or cultural site in the municipality. Evening: find local dining.
assistant.reply.creativeItinerary: Morning: artisan market or opal shop. Afternoon: culinary experience with local dishes. Evening: discover regional festivities.
assistant.reply.teresinaItinerary: Morning: visit museums and the central market. Afternoon: walk by the river park. Evening: try local restaurants.
assistant.reply.regionHintItinerary: Morning: open the territory details on Home. Afternoon: add points in Itinerary. Evening: plan lodging and meals in the region.
assistant.reply.default: Ask about a Piauí destination or topic like food, lodging, or season, and I will answer with practical travel guidance.
assistant.reply.biomes: Piauí highlights four main biomes (Caatinga, Cerrado, Mata dos Cocais, Mata Atlântica). On Home, scroll to the biome section for texts and sample cities.
assistant.reply.delta: The Parnaíba Delta is a coastal icon. On the map you see nearby points; in Itinerary combine beaches and nature with your interests.
assistant.reply.itinerary: In Itinerary, select interests (e.g. environmental education + creative economy) and tap Suggest places. Then refine order and check estimated distance.
assistant.reply.education: For classes and field trips, combine archaeology parks (Origins pole) with coastal nature. The app lists municipalities and categories to build a teaching narrative.
assistant.reply.municipalities: Piauí has 224 municipalities. The map loads boundary layers; territory exploration lives on the Home page.
assistant.reply.capivara: Serra da Capivara hosts world-class archaeological sites. Find the Origins pole on Home and Arqueologia spots in Itinerary.
assistant.reply.creative: Creative economy shows up in crafts, food, and festivals. Open Creative economy in the bottom menu for cards by region.
assistant.reply.teresina: Teresina is the capital pole: museums, rivers, events. See the Teresina card in the territory list on Home.
assistant.reply.regionHint: Your message suggests the {{region}} pole. Open its details on Home and add its points in Itinerary.
assistant.reply.default: Think of me as your travel guide. I can suggest mini-itineraries, the best season to visit, and practical logistics for any Piauí destination.
quiz.q1: How many biomes does Piauí para o Mundo highlight on the home page?
quiz.q2: Where is the Parnaíba Delta mainly placed in the app?
quiz.q3: Serra da Capivara is most associated with which theme?
badge.first_steps: First steps in Piauí para o Mundo
contribute.intro: Log ideas for attractions, events, or teaching materials about the territory.
contribute.title: Short title
contribute.note: In production, this queue would go to a server with moderation before publishing.
contribute.thanks: Thanks! Your suggestion was saved on this device as a demo of the collaborative flow.
